title: Local job matching platform: profiles, job postings, and area-based matching

requirements.text: Implement Internet Identity sign-in and use the authenticated Principal as the account identifier for all user-owned data (profiles, job postings, and applications/matches).

quotes: You asked for a location‑based platform that connects local workers with nearby job providers. We’ll build a simplified version that lets users create profiles, post jobs, and match with workers, using Motoko and React without external map or GPS services.

acceptanceCriteria: Users can sign in/out with Internet Identity in the UI.
Backend endpoints that create/update data associate records with caller Principal.
Users cannot edit or delete other users’ profiles or job postings.

requirements.text: Add worker and job-provider profile management: create, view, and edit basic profile information, including an area field (e.g., city/region/postcode) used for local matching (no GPS/maps).

quotes: ...lets users create profiles... without external map or GPS services.

acceptanceCriteria: A signed-in user can create a profile selecting role: Worker or Provider.
Profile includes at minimum: display name, role, area (free-text), optional bio/contact (non-sensitive, e.g., email/phone as plain text).
A user can view and edit their own profile.
Other users can view a public read-only version of a profile (no edit controls).

requirements.text: Implement job posting functionality for Providers: create, view, edit, close/reopen, and delete job listings; each job includes title, description, area, category/tags, and status.
acceptanceCriteria: Only Provider-role accounts can create job listings.
Providers can edit/close/delete only their own jobs.
Workers can browse open jobs and view job details.
Job list supports filtering by area and category/tags (basic UI filters).

requirements.text: Implement an area-based matching flow between Workers and Jobs without external map/GPS: workers can apply to jobs; providers can review applicants and accept/reject; the system highlights ‘local’ matches when worker area and job area match (exact match or simple normalized match).

quotes: ...match with workers... without external map or GPS services.

acceptanceCriteria: A Worker can apply to an open job once; duplicate applications are prevented.
A Provider can view a list of applicants for each job and see each applicant’s public profile info and area.
A Provider can accept or reject an application; the application status is visible to both parties.
UI indicates when an application is considered ‘local’ based on area matching rules used by the backend.

requirements.text: Persist all core data in the Motoko canister (profiles, jobs, applications) and expose a minimal, typed API to the frontend for CRUD operations and queries (lists/detail views), following the single-actor backend constraint.
acceptanceCriteria: Data remains available across canister upgrades using stable storage patterns appropriate for Motoko.
Backend exposes query methods for lists/details and update methods for create/edit/state changes.
All methods enforce authorization checks based on caller Principal and profile role.

requirements.text: Create a coherent, consistent UI theme for the app (colors, typography, spacing, and component styling) suitable for a local marketplace, and apply it across all pages (avoid blue/purple as the primary palette).
acceptanceCriteria: The UI uses a consistent non-blue/purple primary palette (e.g., warm neutrals with an accent).
Core screens share consistent layout, navigation, and typography.
Shadcn/Tailwind styling is applied consistently (no unstyled default blocks).

requirements.text: Build the main frontend screens and navigation: landing/browse jobs, authentication entry, profile (view/edit), provider job management, job detail + apply, provider applicant review, and worker application status.

quotes: ...create profiles, post jobs, and match with workers...

acceptanceCriteria: Users can navigate between: Browse Jobs, My Profile, and a role-appropriate dashboard (Provider: My Jobs; Worker: My Applications).
Job detail page includes an Apply action for Workers and applicant management for the owning Provider.
All API interactions use React Query for loading/error states and cache invalidation after mutations.

constraints: Use Motoko for backend logic in a single main actor (no multi-canister/microservices).
Do not use external databases or third-party auth providers; only Internet Identity is allowed.
Do not use external map/GPS/location services; matching must use user-entered area text.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui or other immutable paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT; compose them instead.
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Real-time chat or messaging between workers and providers.
GPS-based distance calculations, maps, or geocoding.
Complex ranking/recommendation algorithms beyond simple area-based highlighting.
